page: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 virtual CPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix mathematics.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results place the multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the top tier. The hardware excels in parallel processing tasks, showing strong performance in static web serving, Redis operations, and LLM inference processing. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale enterprise applications, database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge is a high-capacity general-purpose server instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. While it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database operations. Advanced hardware instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, accelerate matrix and cryptographic operations. This resource density and computational capacity make the instance highly efficient for demanding enterprise workloads, large-scale database hosting, high-throughput static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise databases and workloads.

tagline: A high-density x86 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
